flags.concern: Timeline data migration: The plan does not actually achieve its stated "migrate exactly once" invariant on the async load path. `buildTimelineData()` already migrates before resolving, but the shared `resolveTimelineConfig()` helper migrates again internally, and the plan never updates that helper.
flags.evidence: `buildTimelineData()` calls `migrateToFlatTracks(config)` before `resolveTimelineConfig(...)` in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:336] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:339]. The shared resolver then re-runs `migrateToFlatTracks(config)` in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:77] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:82].

flags.concern: Timeline data assembly: The proposed `assembleTimelineData()` centralizes the outer `TimelineData` object literal, but it still leaves `resolvedConfig` and `assetMap` derivation split across multiple callers. That means the plan does not fully deliver on the stated goal of one canonical construction path that guarantees consistent tracks/clips/config by construction.
flags.evidence: Today the sync paths each hand-build `resolvedConfig` and related derived fields in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:30] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:335]. The plan's Step 4 and Step 5 examples keep that manual assembly pattern and only swap the final return object for `assembleTimelineData(...)`. That reduces duplication, but it does not make derivation canonical.

flags.concern: Timeline data validation: The plan refactors the two sync construction paths most closely tied to the duplicate-ID bug (`buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` and `patchRegistry`), but it only proposes existing tests plus manual smoke checks. There is no automated regression coverage for the exact paths being changed.
flags.evidence: The repository has production logic in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:19] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:303], but a repo-wide test search found no tests invoking `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry(...)`, `patchRegistry(...)`, or `useTimelineSave(...)`. Existing tests only cover `configToRows`, `buildTimelineData`, and history/track helpers.
